definitions: To put something down, especially gently or carefully.
To establish or set down as a rule, principle, or law.
To give up or sacrifice one's life, especially for a cause.
To surrender or relinquish something, such as weapons.
To store something for future use, especially wine or provisions.
examples: She will lay down the baby gently.
The committee will lay down new guidelines.
He was willing to lay down his life for his country.
The rebels were ordered to lay down their arms.
They plan to lay down a good vintage for aging.
